| bug-002-worktree-missing-hooks-perms       | bug     | in-progress | P0       | fix/worktree-missing-hooks-perms       | git-agent checkout-feature creates worktrees missing `.claude/hooks/` and lacking a `permissions.allow` block in settings.json — every Write/Edit/MultiEdit call denied at harness layer. 0/10 features completed on kanban-webapp run, $6.52 burned. Found via feat-014 Phase 4 retry on kanban-webapp 2026-04-25.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| bug-003-builder-output-contract-mismatch   | bug     | in-progress | P0       | fix/builder-output-contract-mismatch   | Orchestrator's `translateOutcomes` parser expects `taskOutcomes: { id: status }` map; canonical `BuilderOutput` schema (+ all 3 builder agent prompts) emits `tasksCompleted: BuilderTaskResult[]` array. Result: agent succeeds but is marked failed with "agent produced no parseable outcome JSON". Surfaced cheaply by bug-002's TASK_RETRY_CAP=1 ($1.70 instead of $6) on kanban-webapp 2026-04-25.                                                                                                              |
| bug-004-agent-output-format-schema         | bug     | in-progress | P0       | fix/agent-output-format-schema         | Orchestrator's `buildAgentOptions` never declares `outputFormat` so the SDK runs in plain-text mode; fallback regex `/\{[\s\S]*\}\s*$/` for trailing JSON breaks on markdown fences / trailing prose. Same surface error as bug-003 ("agent produced no parseable outcome JSON") but earlier guard. Fix: use SDK's `outputFormat: { type: 'json_schema', schema: BuilderOutputJsonSchema }` for builder agents. Surfaced at $1.33 on kanban-webapp 2026-04-26.                                                        |
| bug-005-windows-quoting-and-default-branch | bug     | in-progress | P0       | fix/windows-quoting-and-default-branch | TWO bugs surfacing together on Windows+master-default projects: (a) `commitWorktreeChanges` uses `git commit -m '<msg>'` which on cmd.exe parses single quotes as literals → task IDs become pathspecs → every auto-commit fails; (b) `runCloseFeature` hardcodes `main` in 4 git ops → fails on `master`-default projects → falls into phantom merge-conflict retries → emergency-abort. Fix: tempfile-based `git commit -F <path>` + `detectDefaultBranch()` helper. Surfaced at $8.64 on kanban-webapp 2026-04-26. |
| bug-006-greedy-json-extractor              | bug     | in-progress | P0       | fix/greedy-json-extractor              | `extractStructuredOutput`'s greedy regex `/\{[\s\S]*\}\s*$/` matches first `{` to last `}` — when agent prose contains `{` chars (destructuring examples, type defs), it swallows everything between them and the trailing status JSON → JSON.parse fails. Agent succeeded fully (50 tests, 94% coverage, MERGED to master autonomously); orchestrator just couldn't see. Fix: backward-scan from each `{` looking for valid trailing JSON. Surfaced at $1.35 on kanban-webapp 2026-04-26.                            |
